questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider a spongy block of mass $m$ floating on a flowing river. The maximum mass of the block is related to the speed of the river flow $v$, acceleration due to gravity $g$ and the density of the block $\rho$ such that $m_{\max }=k v^x g^y \rho^z(k$ is constant $)$. The values of $x, y$ and $z$ should then respectively be
(Mass of the spongy block is assumed to vary due to absorption of water by it)
questions.solution:
Since, the maximum mass of the block
floating on river depends, speed of flow of the river $=v$, acceleration due to gravity $=g$ and density of the block $=\rho$,
$m_{\max }=k v^x g^y \rho^z$
Compairing the dimensions of $\mathrm{M}, \mathrm{L}$ and $\mathrm{T}$ on both
sides, we get
$z=1$
$\begin{aligned} x+y-3 z & =0 \\ -x-2 y & =0 \\ x+y-3 \times 1 & =0[\text { [From Eq. (i) and (ii)] } \\ x+y & =3\end{aligned}$
From Eqs. (iii) and (iv), we get
$-y=3 \Rightarrow y=-3$
From Eq. (iv), we have
$\begin{aligned} & & x-3 & =3 \\ \Rightarrow & & x & =6\end{aligned}$
Hence, the value of $x, y$ and $z$ will be $(6,-3,1)$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II :

List - I List - II
(a) Gravitational constant (G) (i) L2 T-2
(b) Gravitational potential energy (ii) M-1 L3 T-2
(c) Gravitational potential (iii) LT-2
(d) Gravitational intensity (iv) ML2 T-2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
List - I List - II
(a) Gravitational constant (G) (ii) M-1 L3 T-2
(b) Gravitational potential energy (iv) ML2 T-2
(c) Gravitational potential (i) L2 T-2
(d) Gravitational intensity (iii) LT-2

Hence, (a) - (ii), (b) - (iv), (c) - (i), (d) - (iii).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the energy, $E=G^p h^q c^r$, where $G$ is the universal gravitational constant, $h$ is the Planck's constant and $c$ is the velocity of light, then the values of $p, q$ and $r$ are, respectively
questions.solution:
$E=G^p h^q c^r$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& {\left[\mathrm{M}^1 \mathrm{~L}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2}\right]=\left[\mathrm{M}^{-1} \mathrm{~L}^3 \mathrm{~T}^{-2}\right]^p\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-1}\right]^q\left[\mathrm{LT}^{-1}\right]^r} \\
& =\mathrm{M}^{-p+q} \mathrm{~L}^{3 p+2 q+r} \mathrm{~T}^{-2 p-q-r}
\end{aligned}
$$
Applying principle of homogeneity of dimensions, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& -p+q=1 \\
& 3 p+2 q+r=2 \\
& -2 p-q-r=-2
\end{aligned}
$$
Add (iii) and (iv), $p+q=0$
Add (ii) and (v), we get $q=\frac{1}{2}$
From (ii), we get $p=q-1=\frac{1}{2}-1=-\frac{1}{2}$.
Put in (iii), we get $-\frac{3}{2}+1+r=2, \quad r=5 / 2$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the units of mass, length and time are doubled then unit of angular momentum will be
questions.solution:
The dimensions of angular momentum are

               J=ML2T-1

When units of mass, length and time are doubled, than

             J′=2M2L22T-1

⇒        J′=4ML2T-1=4 J

Unit of angular momentum is quadrupled.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the velocity of light $C$, the gravitational constant $G$ and Planck's constant $h$ are chosen as the fundamental units, the dimension of density in the new system is
questions.solution:
Let $\rho=\mathrm{kC}^{\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{G}^{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{h}^{\mathrm{c}}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad[\rho]=[\mathrm{C}]^{\mathrm{a}}[\mathrm{G}]^{\mathrm{b}}[\mathrm{h}]^{\mathrm{c}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{ML}^{-3}=\mathrm{L}^{\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{T}^{-\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{M}^{-\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{L}^{3 \mathrm{~b}} \mathrm{~T}^{-2 \mathrm{~b}} \mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{c}} \mathrm{L}^{2 \mathrm{c}} \mathrm{T}^{-\mathrm{c}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 1=-\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c},-3=\mathrm{a}+3 \mathrm{~b}+2 \mathrm{c}, 0=-\mathrm{a}-2 \mathrm{~b}-\mathrm{c} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{a}=5, \mathrm{~b}=-2 \text { and } \mathrm{c}=-1
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) Energy per unit volume and angular momentum can be added dimensionally.
Reason (R) Physical quantities having same dimensions can be added or subtracted.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
The units of energy per unit volume is $\mathrm{J} / \mathrm{m}^3$ or $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}-\mathrm{s}^2$ and that for angular momentum is $\mathrm{kg}-\mathrm{m}^2 / \mathrm{s}$.
Since, they are not dimensionally equal, they cannot be added, so the Assertion (A) is false. Only those physical quantities that have same dimensions can be added or subtracted, so Reason (R) is true.
Hence, the correct option is $(\mathrm{d})$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> and acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math> are chosen as the fundamental physical quantities, then the dimension of Young's modulus in terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math> are
questions.solution:
Let Y=fV, F, a

∴Y=KVxFyaz,      K→Unitless

∴Y=VxFyaz

∴ML-1T-2=LT-1xMLT-2yLT-2z

∴ML-1=T-2= MyLx+y+zT-x-2y-2z 

∴y=1;    x+y+z=-1;    -x-2y-2z=2

∴x+z=-2           ∴x+2y+2z=2

∴x+2z=0              ∴z=2, x=-4

∴Y=V-4Fa2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $A$ represents Boltzmann constant, $B$ represents Planck's constant and $C$ represents speed of light in vacuum, then the quantity having the dimensions of $A^4 B^{-3} C^{-2}$ is
questions.solution:
Dimension of Boltzmann's constant
$$
A=\frac{\mathrm{J}}{\mathrm{K}}=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2}}{\mathrm{~K}^1}=\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right]
$$

Dimension of Planck constant
$$
B=\mathrm{Js}=\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-1}\right]
$$

Dimension of speed of light,
$$
C=\left[\mathrm{LT}^{-1}\right]
$$

Desired dimension $=A^4 B^{-3} C^{-2}$
$\Rightarrow\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right]^4\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-1}\right]^{-3}\left[\mathrm{LT}^{-1}\right]^{-2}$
$\Rightarrow\left[\mathrm{M}^1 \mathrm{~L}^0 \mathrm{~T}^{-3} \mathrm{~K}^{-4}\right]$
Stefan constant $(\sigma)$ is given by
$$
E=\sigma T^4 \Rightarrow \sigma=\frac{E}{T^4}
$$
where, $E=$ energy per unit area per unit time and $\quad T=$ absolute temperature.
$\Rightarrow$ Dimension, $\sigma=\frac{\left[\mathrm{M} \mathrm{L}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2}\right]}{\left.\left[\mathrm{L}^2\right][\mathrm{T}] \mathrm{K}^4\right]}$
$$
\sigma=\left[\mathrm{ML}^0 \mathrm{~T}^{-3} \mathrm{~K}^{-4}\right]
$$

So, desired dimension is of Stefan constant.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the dimensions of a physical quantity are given by $\left[\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{L}^{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{T}^{\mathrm{c}}\right]$, then the physical quantity will be :
questions.solution: $[\mathrm{P}]=\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{A}}=\left[\frac{\mathrm{MLT}^{-2}}{\mathrm{~L}^2}\right]=\left[\mathrm{ML}^{-1} \mathrm{~T}^{-2}\right]$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A certain physical quantity is calculated from the formula $\frac{\pi}{3}\left(a^2-b^2\right) h d$, where $a, b$ and $h$ are all lengths and $d$ is density. The physical quantity being calculated is
questions.solution:
$\frac{\pi}{3}\left(a^2-b^2\right)$ hd   ...(1)
a, b, h has length "l" so there unit is $\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L})$ $\mathrm{d}=$ density $=\frac{\text { Mass }}{\text { volume }}=\frac{\mathrm{kg}}{\mathrm{m}^3}=\frac{\mathrm{M}}{\mathrm{L}^3}$
$\frac{\pi}{3}$ has no dimensions 
from equation (1)
Now, $\left(\mathrm{L}^2-\mathrm{L}^2\right) \frac{\mathrm{M}}{\mathrm{L}^3}(\mathrm{~L})$
$\frac{\mathrm{L}^3 \mathrm{M}}{\mathrm{L}^3}=\mathrm{M} \Rightarrow$ Mass
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is the unit of mobility of a electron in a conductor?
questions.solution:
Mobility is given as $\mu=\frac{e \tau}{\mathrm{m}}$
So, unit of $\mu$ is $\frac{\text { coulomb }-\mathrm{sec}}{\mathrm{kg}}$ i.e. $\mathrm{kg}^{-1} \mathrm{~s}^2 \mathrm{~A}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The dimensions of <math><mfrac><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>, in the equation <math><mi>P</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is pressure, <math><mi>x</mi></math> is distance and <math><mi>t</mi></math> is time, are
questions.solution:
P=a-t2bx

⇒   Pbx=a-t2

⇒   Pbx=a=t2

Or b= t2P x=T2ML-1T-2 L=M-1T4

∴    ab= T2M-1T4=MT-2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The velocity of water waves <math> <mi>v</mi> </math> may depend upon their wavelength <math> <mi>λ</mi> </math> , the density of water <math> <mi>ρ</mi> </math> and the acceleration due to gravity <math> <mi>g</mi> </math> . The method of dimensions gives the relation between these quantities as
questions.solution: Let v x =k g y λ z ρ δ . Now by substituting the dimensions of each quantities and equating the powers of M, L and T we get δ=0 and x=2, y=1, z=1 .
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The speed of ripples <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>v</mi><mo>)</mo></math> on water surface depends on surface tension <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>σ</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>ρ</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, and wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>λ</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. Then the square of speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>v</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is proportional to
questions.solution:
Speed of ripples, v depends on σ,ρ,λ

⇒v∝σaρbλc

⇒M0LT-1∝MT-2aML-3bLc

⇒M0LT-1∝Ma+bL-3b+cT-2a

By equating the powers, ⇒a+b=0, -3b+c=1, -2a=-1

⇒a=12, b=-12, c=-12

⇒v∝σ12ρ-12λ-12

⇒v∝σρλ⇒v2∝σρλ
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In a new system of units, the unit of force is <math> <mrow> <mtext>100 N</mtext></mrow></math>, unit of length is <math> <mrow> <mtext>10 m</mtext></mrow> </math> and unit of time is <math> <mrow> <mtext>100 s</mtext></mrow></math>. Unit of mass in this system can be expressed as
questions.solution:
Here, force (F) = [MLT–2] = 100 N             ...(i)

Length (L) = [L] = 10 m             ...(ii)

Time (t) = [T] = 100 s             ...(iii)

Substituting values of L and T from Equations. (ii) and (iii) in Equation. (i), we get

                   M × 10 × (100)–2 = 100

or                    M⁡ × 1 0 1 0 0 × 1 0 0 = 1 0 0

or                   M = 100 × 1000 kg

                    M= 10 5  kg
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The expressions below give current $I$ through an electronic component as a function of applied potential $V . I_0$ and $V_0$ are constants having dimensions of current and potential respectively. Which of the following are dimensionally incorrect?
(A) $I=I_0\left(e^{\frac{2 V}{V_0}}+1\right)$
(B) $I=I_0\left(e^{\frac{v}{2 V_0}}-1\right)$
(C) $I=I_0 V_0\left(e^{\frac{V}{V_0}}-1\right)$
(D) $I=I_0\left(\frac{V}{V_0}\right)\left(e^{\frac{V}{V_0}}-1\right)$
questions.solution:
According to the principle of homogeneity, a physical equation will be dimensionally correct only if the dimensions of all the terms occuring on both side of the equation are the same.
Hence, in option (c)
$$
I=I_0 V_0\left(e^{\frac{v}{V_0}}-1\right)
$$
Dimension of $I \neq$ dimensions of $I_0 V_0$ Therefore, equation shown in option (c) is not dimensionally correct.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Number of particles, given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>D</mi><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>n</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>n</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></math>, is crossing a unit area perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis in unit time, where <math><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> are number of particles per unit volume for the value of <math><mi>x</mi></math> meant to <math><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> . Find dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math>, called as diffusion constant.
questions.solution:
Given, number of particle passing from unit area in unit time =n

=Number of particleA×t=M0 L0 T0L2 T=L-2 T-1

n1=n2=Number of particle in unit volume=L-3

Now, from the given formula D=n x2-x1n2-n1

=L-2 T-1 LL-3

=L2 T-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In a particular system, the unit of length, mass and time are chosen to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> respectively. The unit of force in this system will be equivalent to -
questions.solution:
Dimensionally

F=MLT-2

In C.G.S. system

1 dyne=1g 1 cm 1s-2

In the new system

1x=10 g10 cm0.1 s-2

1 dyne1 x=1 g10 g×1 cm10 cm10 s1 s-2

1 dyne=110,000×1x

104 dyne=1x

10x=105 dyne=1 N

x=110 N
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If dimensions of critical velocity, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></msub></math> of a liquid flowing through a tube are expressed as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">η</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msup><msup><mi>r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>, where, <math><mi mathvariant="normal">η</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi></math> and <math> <mi>r</mi> </math> are the coefficient of viscosity of liquid, density of liquid and radius of the tube, respectively, then, the values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math> and <math> <mtext>z</mtext> </math> are given by
questions.solution:
Dimensions of velocity, coefficient of viscosity and density are,

v=LT-1

η=FAdvdx=MLT-2L2T-1=ML-1T-1

ρ=ML-3

Now, v=ηxρyrz

 ⇒LT-1=ML-1T-1xML-3yLz

Now, by using dimensional homogeneity, we will get,

M0=Mx+y

⇒y=-x

For T, 

x=1

⇒y=-x=-1

For L,

1=-x-3y+z

⇒z=-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The dependency of speed of water surface waves (capillary waves) on the density of water <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math> their wavelength <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">λ</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and surface tension <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">γ</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is -
questions.solution:
Velocity of surface waves depends on density of water, its own wavelength of surface tension.

v∝ρaλbγc

LT-1∝ML-3aLbMLT-2Lc

LT-1∝Ma+cL-3a+bT-2c

a+c=0                 ..........(i)

-3a+b=1           .............(ii)


-2c=-1               .............(iii)

Solving
 a=-12 , b=-12 , c=12 ,

v∝ρ-12λ-12γ12

v∝γρλ
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
